Document:
# Medicine
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Medicine"]
Medicine and chems are essential for surviving the Wasteland. Knowing what to use and when can mean the difference between life and death. Most medical supplies can be found in first aid kits, looted from enemies, or purchased from traveling doctors and merchants — but the craftable ones can mean the difference between life and death when you're far from civilization.
## Tools
A mortar and pestle is required to process ingredients for many medical recipes. Craft one from two wooden planks.
[bold]Mortar and Pestle[/bold] — Crafting tool for grinding plants and mixing powders.
Ingredients: 2x Wood Plank
## Wound Care
[color=#a4885c]Dirty gauze[/color] is a last-resort wound dressing. It stops bleeding but carries a risk of infection. Craft it from cloth strips.
[bold]Dirty Gauze[/bold] — Emergency bandage. Stops bleeding but may cause infection.
Ingredients: 2x Cloth
[color=#a4885c]Clean gauze[/color] is usually found in first aid kits and medical caches. [color=#a4885c]Ointment[/color] treats burns and cold damage, and can either be scavenged or brewed in the field from agave nectar, clean water, and ash.
[bold]Clean Gauze[/bold] — Sterile bandage. Safe and effective wound treatment.
[bold]Ointment[/bold] — Topical treatment for burns and exposure damage.
Ointment reagents: Agave Nectar + Water + Ash
## Healing Powder
A tribal remedy that accelerates natural healing. Nauseating but effective. Craft it from broc flower and xander root — no workbench required, just crush them together.
[bold]Healing Powder[/bold] — Tribal herbal remedy. Heals over time but causes nausea.
Ingredients: 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root
## Cleansing Powder
A field remedy used by tribal and Legion healers to purge radiation. Craft it from one irradiated cave fungus and one xander root in the medical tab of the crafting menu.
[bold]Cleansing Powder[/bold] — Anti-radiation herbal treatment.
Ingredients: 1x Irradiated Cave Fungus + 1x Xander Root
## Healing Poultice
A more advanced natural remedy combining multiple plant extracts. Heals common ailments slowly but surpasses healing powder for sustained recovery.
[bold]Healing Poultice[/bold] — Advanced herbal remedy for sustained healing.
Ingredients: 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root + 1x Cave Fungus + 1x Agave
## Stimpaks
[color=#a4885c]Stimpaks[/color] are the backbone of Wasteland medicine — injectable serums that rapidly accelerate the body's healing. Craft a standard stimpak by combining broc flower and xander root with a syringe and a bloodpack. If you are working from loose reagents, the herbal base is first mixed into healing mixture and then stabilized with oil and phosphorus to finish the syringe.
[bold]Stimpak[/bold] — Instant healing injection. The most important medical item in the Wasteland.
Ingredients: 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root + 1x Syringe + 1x Bloodpack
Chemistry intermediates: Healing Mixture, Dirty Stimpak
[color=#a4885c]Dirty stimpaks[/color] can also be improvised with wasteland blood if you are desperate. [color=#a4885c]Super stimpaks[/color] remain pre-war or professionally manufactured goods sold by doctors and traders.
## Bitterdrink
A bottled tribal remedy for poison and radiation exposure. In practice, it is brewed from broc flower and xander root and poured into an empty soda bottle.
[bold]Bitterdrink[/bold] — Tribal anti-poison and anti-radiation drink.
Ingredients: 1x Broc Flower + 1x Xander Root + 1x Empty Soda Bottle
Chemistry station note: if you are working in a proper lab, you can print an empty bitterdrink bottle and fill it through the ChemMaster instead of hand-assembling it from a scavenged soda bottle.
## Jet Inhaler
A Jet inhaler is a crafting component used to make a RadAway inhaler. The crafting recipe makes the empty inhaler body from a sheet of plastic.
[bold]Jet Inhaler[/bold] — Empty inhaler body. Used as a component for RadAway inhalers.
Ingredients: 1x Sheet Plastic
## Hydra
Hydra requires chemistry first. Mix radscorpion tail, cave fungus extract, and nightstalker blood at a chemistry set to produce Hydra reagent (2 units per batch — you need 8 batches to reach the 15u required). Once you have 15u of Hydra, pour it into an empty hydra inhaler, then seal it with a screwdriver in the medical tab of the crafting menu.
[bold]Hydra[/bold] — Powerful healing chem that repairs crippled limbs over time.
Chemistry reagents (per batch, yields 2u): Scorpion Tail + Fungus Extract + Nightstalker Blood
Assembly: Fill Empty Hydra Inhaler with 15u Hydra, then seal with screwdriver.
## RadAway Inhaler
Flushes radiation from the bloodstream. Assemble the inhaler body first (see Jet Inhaler above), then attach a RadAway phial loaded with RadAway reagent.
[bold]RadAway Inhaler[/bold] — Primary radiation treatment. Flushes rads from the bloodstream.
Assembly: Jet Inhaler + RadAway Phial
[color=#a4885c]RadAway[/color] itself is mixed from irradiated fungus extract, antiseptic, and water. If supplies are tight, you can water it down into a weaker diluted RadAway.
Chemistry: RadAway and Diluted RadAway are both available as reagents.
[color=#a4885c]RadAway phials[/color] and [color=#a4885c]RadAway bloodbags[/color] are also found as standalone items throughout the Wasteland.
## Antidotes and Other Remedies
Most finished items in this section are found or purchased — looted from enemies, bought from traveling doctors, or discovered in medical caches across the Wasteland. The chemistry itself can still be refined if you have the right ingredients.
[color=#a4885c]Antidote chemistry[/color] starts with radscorpion tail and ethanol to make antidote mixture, then uses tarberries to refine it into proper antidote.
Chemistry: Antidote Mixture → Antidote (refined with tarberries)
Not every item in this section is a straightforward treatment. Mourning poultice is included here because it is a field compound you may find alongside medical supplies, but its purpose is ritual rather than restorative. It is primarily associated with Legion and tribal ceremonial use: a poisonous herbal mixture meant to induce visions and altered speech, carrying clear negative connotations and causing minor physical harm instead of healing.
[bold]Antidote[/bold] — Cures most poisons and venoms.
[bold]Smelling Salts[/bold] — Revives unconscious patients. Useful for field triage.
[bold]Mourning Poultice[/bold] — Ritual compound. Causes visions and minor harm. Not a medicine.
## Pills, Canisters, and Chems
Pills and canisters are pre-manufactured and cannot be crafted. They are found in medical caches, purchased from doctors, or looted from enemies.
[bold]Rad-X[/bold] — Radiation resistance. Take before entering contaminated areas.
[bold]Buffout[/bold] — Increases strength and endurance temporarily. Risk of addiction.
[bold]Mentats[/bold] — Boosts intelligence and perception. Risk of addiction.
[bold]Fixer[/bold] — Treats most chem addictions. Limited availability.
[bold]Cateye[/bold] — Grants night vision temporarily.
[bold]Jet[/bold] — Speeds up perception and reaction time. Highly addictive.
[bold]Hydra[/bold] — Repairs crippled limbs over time.
[bold]Buffjet[/bold] — Combination of Buffout and Jet effects. Very addictive.
[bold]Bufftats[/bold] — Combination of Buffout and Mentats effects. Moderate addiction risk.
[color=#f44336][bold]Warning:[/bold][/color] Prolonged chem use leads to addiction. Fixer can treat most addictions, but availability is not guaranteed.
